94	    def unpack(self, fmt: str) -> tuple:

149	    size = ARG_SIZE[arg_type]
150	    if size == 0: return None
151	    if arg_type == ArgType.INTEGER: return r.read_i32()
152	    if arg_type == ArgType.REAL: return r.read_f32()
153	    if arg_type == ArgType.BOOLEAN: return r.read_bool()
154	    if arg_type in (ArgType.OBJECTID, ArgType.DRAWABLEID, ArgType.TEAMID, ArgType.TIMESTAMP):
155	        return r.read_u32()
156	    if arg_type == ArgType.LOCATION:
157	        x, y, z = r.unpack("<3f"); return (x, y, z)
158	    if arg_type == ArgType.PIXEL:
159	        x, y = r.unpack("<2i"); return (x, y)
160	    if arg_type == ArgType.PIXELREGION:
161	        x1, y1, x2, y2 = r.unpack("<4i"); return (x1, y1, x2, y2)

167	def _parse_commands(r: _BinaryReader) -> list[ReplayCommand]:
168	    """Parse the command stream.
169	
170	    The on-disk format stores declarations before data:
171	        frame(u32) msg_type(u32) player_idx(i32) num_types(u8)
172	        [num_types × (arg_type(u8)  arg_count(u8))]   ← all declarations first
173	        [data for decl0 … data for decl_N]             ← all payloads after
174	    """
175	    commands: list[ReplayCommand] = []
176	    while r.remaining >= 4:
177	        try:
178	            frame = r.read_u32()
179	        except EOFError:
180	            break
181	        if r.remaining < 9: break
182	        try:
183	            msg_type = r.read_u32(); player_idx = r.read_i32(); num_types = r.read_u8()
184	        except EOFError:
185	            break
186	        # Validate msg_type range; anything outside signals stream corruption / EOF
187	        if msg_type == MSG_CLEAR_GAME_DATA and num_types == 0: break
188	        if not (MSG_NET_BEGIN <= msg_type <= MSG_NET_END) and msg_type != MSG_CLEAR_GAME_DATA:
189	            break
190	        # Phase 1: read all (type, count) declarations
191	        decls: list[tuple[ArgType, int]] = []
192	        truncated = False
193	        for _ in range(num_types):
194	            if r.remaining < 2: truncated = True; break
195	            raw_type = r.read_u8(); arg_count = r.read_u8()
196	            try:
197	                arg_type = ArgType(raw_type)
198	            except ValueError:
199	                truncated = True; break
200	            decls.append((arg_type, arg_count))
201	        if truncated:
202	            break
203	        # Phase 2: read all payload data in declaration order
204	        args: dict[ArgType, list[Any]] = {}
205	        for arg_type, arg_count in decls:
206	            try:
207	                args[arg_type] = [_parse_arg(r, arg_type) for _ in range(arg_count)]
208	            except EOFError:
209	                truncated = True; break
210	        if truncated:
211	            break
212	        commands.append(ReplayCommand(frame=frame, msg_type=msg_type, player_idx=player_idx, args=args))
213	    return commands
214

216	def parse_replay_bytes(data: bytes) -> ReplayFile:
217	    if not data:
218	        raise ValueError("Empty input")
219	    r = _BinaryReader(data)
220	    return ReplayFile(header=_parse_header(r), commands=_parse_commands(r))

52	def parse_game_options(options_str: str) -> GameInfo:
53	    """Parse the semicolon-delimited game options string embedded in the header.
54	
55	    Format (observed from real ZH .rep files):
56	        US=1;M=<2-char-prefix><map_path>;...;S=<slot0>:<slot1>:...:<slotN>:;
57	    Each slot (non-empty, non-'X') has the form:
58	        H/C<name>,<hex>,<hex>,<faction_code>,<team>,<color>,<...>
59	    where H = human, C = CPU.
60	    """
61	    info = GameInfo(raw=options_str)
62	    if not options_str:
63	        return info
64	
65	    # Split on semicolons; build a key→value dict for the simple keys
66	    kv: dict[str, str] = {}
67	    for token in options_str.split(";"):
68	        if "=" in token:
69	            key, _, value = token.partition("=")
70	            kv[key.strip()] = value.strip()
71	
72	    # Map path: "M" value starts with a 2-character hex/type prefix
73	    raw_map = kv.get("M", "")
74	    info.map_path = raw_map[2:] if len(raw_map) > 2 else raw_map
75	
76	    # Player slots: "S" value is colon-separated slot descriptors
77	    raw_slots = kv.get("S", "")
78	    slot_number = 0
79	    for raw_slot in raw_slots.split(":"):
80	        raw_slot = raw_slot.strip()
81	        if not raw_slot or raw_slot == "X":
82	            slot_number += 1
83	            continue
84	        fields = raw_slot.split(",")
85	        if not fields:
86	            slot_number += 1
87	            continue
88	        name_field = fields[0]
89	        is_human = name_field.startswith("H")
90	        name = name_field[1:] if name_field else ""
91	        faction_code = fields[3] if len(fields) > 3 else ""
92	        faction = _decode_faction(faction_code)
93	        try:
94	            team = int(fields[4]) if len(fields) > 4 else 0
95	        except ValueError:
96	            team = 0
97	        info.slots.append(PlayerSlot(
98	            slot_number=slot_number,
99	            name=name,
100	            faction=faction,
101	            is_human=is_human,
102	            team=team,
103	        ))
104	        slot_number += 1
105	
106	    return info

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from zh_copilot.replay.parser import ReplayFile
from zh_copilot.replay.game_options import parse_game_options

MIN_DURATION_SECONDS = 60.0
MIN_COMMANDS = 20


@dataclass
class ReplayStats:
    duration_seconds: float
    total_commands: int
    commands_per_player: dict[int, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
    num_players: int = 0
    map_path: str = ""
    factions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    version_number: int = 0
    desync_game: bool = False
    quit_early: bool = False


def compute_stats(replay: ReplayFile) -> ReplayStats:
    gameplay_cmds = replay.gameplay_commands
    per_player: dict[int, int] = {}
    for cmd in gameplay_cmds:
        per_player[cmd.player_idx] = per_player.get(cmd.player_idx, 0) + 1
    game_info = parse_game_options(replay.header.game_options)
    return ReplayStats(
        duration_seconds=replay.header.duration_seconds,
        total_commands=len(gameplay_cmds),
        commands_per_player=per_player,
        num_players=game_info.num_players,
        map_path=game_info.map_path,
        factions=game_info.factions,
        version_number=replay.header.version_number,
        desync_game=replay.header.desync_game,
        quit_early=replay.header.quit_early,
    )


def is_valid_for_training(replay: ReplayFile, min_duration: float = MIN_DURATION_SECONDS, min_commands: int = MIN_COMMANDS) -> bool:
    if replay.header.desync_game: return False
    if replay.header.duration_seconds < min_duration: return False
    if any(replay.header.player_discons): return False
    if len(replay.gameplay_commands) < min_commands: return False
    return True


def filter_replays(replays: list[ReplayFile], min_duration: float = MIN_DURATION_SECONDS, min_commands: int = MIN_COMMANDS) -> list[ReplayFile]:
    return [r for r in replays if is_valid_for_training(r, min_duration, min_commands)]

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/4thwithme/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.6/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec self-review** — quick inline check for placeholders, contradictions, ambiguity, scope (see below)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" [shape=box];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)";
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" -> "User reviews spec?";
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Self-Review:**
After writing the spec document, look at it with fresh eyes:

1. **Placeholder scan:** Any "TBD", "TODO", incomplete sections, or vague requirements? Fix them.
2. **Internal consistency:** Do any sections contradict each other? Does the architecture match the feature descriptions?
3. **Scope check:** Is this focused enough for a single implementation plan, or does it need decomposition?
4. **Ambiguity check:** Could any requirement be interpreted two different ways? If so, pick one and make it explicit.

Fix any issues inline. No need to re-review — just fix and move on.

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

# Plan 2: Replay Parser & Dataset Pipeline

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Parse C&C Generals Zero Hour `.rep` replay files into structured Python data — command sequences, player info, game options. Then (on Intel Mac) replay them through the headless engine to generate `(state, action, outcome)` training datasets.

**Architecture:** Pure Python parser reads `.rep` binary format → `ReplayFile` dataclass. A separate `ReplayRunner` (engine-dependent, Intel Mac only) replays commands through the headless engine and records `GameStateObservation` at each decision step. Datasets are stored as HDF5 files.

**Tech Stack:** Python 3.11+, `struct`, `h5py`, `numpy`, `pyarrow` (Parquet), `tqdm`, `pytest`

**M4 Mac scope:** Tasks 1–5 (parser, game options, analysis tools, collection scripts, tests).
**Intel Mac scope:** Tasks 6–7 (replay runner, dataset generation — requires Plan 1 engine).

---

## File Structure

```
src/zh_copilot/replay/
├── __init__.py
├── parser.py           # Binary .rep parser → ReplayFile dataclass
├── game_options.py     # gameOptions ASCII string sub-parser → GameInfo

## .rep File Format Reference

### Header (fixed section, bytes 0–27)

| Offset | Field | Type | Size |
|--------|-------|------|------|
| 0x00 | magic | ASCII | 6 bytes — `"GENREP"` |
| 0x06 | start_time | uint32 | 4 bytes — Unix timestamp |
| 0x0A | end_time | uint32 | 4 bytes — Unix timestamp |
| 0x0E | frame_duration | uint32 | 4 bytes — total logic frames |
| 0x12 | desync_game | bool | 1 byte |
| 0x13 | quit_early | bool | 1 byte |
| 0x14 | player_discons[0..7] | bool×8 | 8 bytes |

### Header (variable section, immediately after byte 27)

| Field | Encoding |
|-------|----------|
| replay_name | Null-terminated UTF-16LE |
| time_val | 16 bytes — SYSTEMTIME (8×uint16: year, month, dow, day, hour, min, sec, ms) |
| version_string | Null-terminated UTF-16LE |
| version_time_string | Null-terminated UTF-16LE |
| version_number | uint32 |
| exe_crc | uint32 |
| ini_crc | uint32 |
| game_options | Null-terminated ASCII |
| local_player_index | Null-terminated ASCII (decimal integer string) |
| difficulty | int32 |
| original_game_mode | int32 |
| rank_points | int32 |
| max_fps | int32 |

### Command stream (immediately after header)

content.text:
Three approaches:

---

**A) Two standalone scripts in `scripts/`**
- `scripts/analyze_replays.py` + `scripts/create_batches.py`
- No package structure, just run directly
- Con: not importable by training pipeline — you'd have to re-parse or shell out

---

**B) Modules in `src/zh_copilot/data/` + thin CLI wrappers** ← recommended
- `src/zh_copilot/data/metadata.py` — `ReplayMetadata` dataclass + scanner
- `src/zh_copilot/data/batcher.py` — `StratifiedBatcher` class
- `scripts/analyze_replays.py` and `scripts/create_batches.py` are thin entry points that call the modules
- Pro: training pipeline can `import` the batcher directly later; testable; fits project conventions
- Con: slightly more structure upfront

---

**C) Extend `replay/analysis.py` + add `replay/batcher.py`**
- Keep everything in the `replay/` module
- Pro: metadata extraction is logically "replay analysis"
- Con: mixes replay parsing concerns with data pipeline concerns; `analysis.py` already does stats/filtering, batcher is a different responsibility

---

**Recommendation: B.** The batcher will be needed by the training pipeline (`ILTrainer`) eventually — it needs to be importable, not just a script. And `data/` is already the right module for pipeline-level concerns (downloader, index are there). Fits the pattern.

content.text:
**Design — Script 2: `scripts/create_batches.py`**

Reads `data/replay_metadata.json`, takes only `valid: true` records, stratifies across all 5 dimensions, outputs batches.

**Stratification logic:**
1. Build a `stratum_key` per replay: `f"{matchup}__{duration_bucket}__{map_bucket}__{game_mode}"`
   - `map_bucket` — top-N most common maps by name, rest binned as `"other"`
2. Sort strata by frequency, assign replays round-robin across batches — guarantees each batch has roughly equal representation of every stratum
3. Any stratum with too few replays to fill a batch gets distributed evenly rather than skipped

**CLI args:**
```
--metadata   path to replay_metadata.json  [default: data/replay_metadata.json]
--output     path to write batches JSON    [default: data/batches.json]
--batch-size N                             [default: 256]
--top-maps   N  top maps to keep as own bucket [default: 20]
--seed       int for reproducibility       [default: 42]
```

**Output format `data/batches.json`:**
```json
{
  "generated_at": "...",
  "batch_size": 256,
  "num_batches": 140,
  "strata_counts": {"GLA_vs_GLA__long__canyon_of_the_dead__1": 412, ...},
  "batches": [
    {"batch_id": 0, "replay_ids": ["369445", "369389", ...]},
    ...
  ]
}
```

Each batch has roughly equal stratum mix. `replay_ids` are the filename stems so the training loop can resolve `replays/dataset/<id>.rep`.

import pytest
from zh_copilot.replay.analysis import ReplayStats, compute_stats, is_valid_for_training, filter_replays
from zh_copilot.replay.parser import ReplayFile, ReplayHeader, ReplayCommand, SystemTime
from zh_copilot.replay.message_types import MsgType


def make_header(frame_duration=9000, desync=False, quit_early=False) -> ReplayHeader:
    return ReplayHeader(
        magic="GENREP", start_time=0, end_time=300,
        frame_duration=frame_duration, desync_game=desync, quit_early=quit_early,
        player_discons=[False]*8, replay_name="test",
        time_val=SystemTime(2024,1,1,1,0,0,0,0),
        version_string="1.04", version_time_string="", version_number=10401,
        exe_crc=0, ini_crc=0,
        game_options="M=maps/alpine.map C=1 F=America H=1 T=1 C=2 F=China H=1 T=2",
        local_player_index=0, difficulty=1, original_game_mode=7,
        rank_points=0, max_fps=30,
    )


def make_command(frame, msg_type, player_idx):
    return ReplayCommand(frame=frame, msg_type=msg_type, player_idx=player_idx)


def test_compute_stats_duration():
    header = make_header(frame_duration=9000)
    commands = [make_command(f*300, MsgType.DO_MOVETO, 0) for f in range(30)]
    stats = compute_stats(ReplayFile(header=header, commands=commands))
    assert stats.duration_seconds == pytest.approx(300.0)


def test_compute_stats_command_counts():
    header = make_header()
    commands = [
        make_command(100, MsgType.DO_MOVETO, 0),
        make_command(200, MsgType.DO_MOVETO, 1),
        make_command(300, MsgType.DOZER_CONSTRUCT, 0),
    ]
    stats = compute_stats(ReplayFile(header=header, commands=commands))
    assert stats.total_commands == 3
    assert stats.commands_per_player[0] == 2
    assert stats.commands_per_player[1] == 1


def test_valid_for_training_normal():
    header = make_header(frame_duration=9000)
    commands = [make_command(f*30, MsgType.DO_MOVETO, f%2) for f in range(100)]
    assert is_valid_for_training(ReplayFile(header=header, commands=commands)) is True


def test_invalid_desync():
    assert is_valid_for_training(ReplayFile(header=make_header(desync=True), commands=[])) is False


def test_invalid_too_short():
    assert is_valid_for_training(ReplayFile(header=make_header(frame_duration=600), commands=[])) is False


def test_filter_replays():
    good = ReplayFile(header=make_header(9000), commands=[make_command(f*30, MsgType.DO_MOVETO, f%2) for f in range(50)])
    bad = ReplayFile(header=make_header(desync=True), commands=[])
    result = filter_replays([good, bad])
    assert len(result) == 1
    assert result[0] is good

# Replay Metadata & Stratified Batching — Design

> Created: 2026-04-03
> Phase: 2b (M4 Mac scope)
> Status: Approved

---

## Problem

35,998 downloaded `.rep` files need to be analyzed before training so that the
`ILTrainer` can draw balanced mini-batches across faction matchups, game
durations, maps, and game modes. Without stratification the trainer will
overfit to GLA-heavy, long-game, single-map replays (the majority in the
GameReplays.org dataset).

---

## Solution

Two standalone scripts that form a pre-training data preparation pipeline:

```
replays/dataset/*.rep
        │
        ▼
scripts/analyze_replays.py  ─►  data/replay_metadata.json
                                        │
                                        ▼
                              scripts/create_batches.py  ─►  data/batches.json
```

Both scripts are self-contained — they add `src/` to `sys.path` and import
`zh_copilot.replay.*` directly. No package installation required.

---

## Script 1 — `scripts/analyze_replays.py`

### What it does

Scans a directory of `.rep` files, parses each one, and writes a JSON metadata
index.

### CLI

```
python scripts/analyze_replays.py [--replays replays/dataset] [--output data/replay_metadata.json] [--force]
```

- `--replays`  Directory containing `.rep` files (default: `replays/dataset`)
- `--output`   Output path (default: `data/replay_metadata.json`)
- `--force`    Re-analyze all files even if already indexed

### Metadata record schema

| Field            | Type         | Description |
|------------------|--------------|-------------|
| `replay_id`      | str          | File stem (e.g. `"369445"`) |
| `path`           | str          | Relative path to `.rep` file |
| `md5`            | str          | MD5 hex digest of raw file bytes |
| `duration_s`     | float        | Game length in seconds |
| `duration_bucket`| str          | `"short"` <5min / `"medium"` 5–20min / `"long"` >20min |
| `game_mode`      | int          | `original_game_mode` from header |
| `num_players`    | int          | Human slot count |
| `is_1v1`         | bool         | True if exactly 2 human players |
| `map_path`       | str          | Normalized map name from game options |
| `factions`       | list[str]    | Faction per human player |
| `matchup`        | str          | Sorted canonical key e.g. `"America_vs_GLA"` |
| `version`        | int          | `version_number` from header |
| `valid`          | bool         | Passes `is_valid_for_training` filter |
| `invalid_reason` | str \| null  | Reason if invalid: `"desync"`, `"too_short"`, `"disconnection"`, `"too_few_commands"`, `"duplicate"`, `"parse_error:<msg>"` |

### Output file shape

```json
{
  "generated_at": "2026-04-03T10:00:00+00:00",
  "replays_dir": "replays/dataset",
  "total": 35998,
  "valid": 31240,
  "replays": [ ... ]
}
```

### Deduplication

After scanning, any record whose `md5` appears more than once is marked
`valid: false`, `invalid_reason: "duplicate"` — only the first occurrence
(lowest replay_id) is kept valid. The metadata file is the single source of
truth for dedup.

### Resumption

Without `--force`, already-indexed `replay_id` values are skipped. The script
merges new records into an existing output file.

---

## Script 2 — `scripts/create_batches.py`

### What it does

Reads `data/replay_metadata.json`, takes only `valid: true` records, and
distributes them into stratified batches that each contain proportional
coverage of every stratum.

### CLI

```
python scripts/create_batches.py [--metadata data/replay_metadata.json] [--output data/batches.json] [--batch-size 256] [--top-maps 20] [--seed 42]
```

### Stratum key

```
{matchup}__{duration_bucket}__{map_bucket}__{game_mode}
```

- `map_bucket`: top-N most frequent map names kept as-is; remainder → `"other"`
- Map name is the final path component, lowercased, with `[rank]` prefix stripped

### Stratification algorithm

1. Shuffle IDs within each stratum (seeded for reproducibility)
2. For each stratum, assign `ids[i]` to `batches[i % num_batches]` — guarantees
   proportional representation regardless of stratum size
3. `num_batches = len(valid_replays) // batch_size`

### Output file shape

```json
{
  "generated_at": "2026-04-03T10:00:00+00:00",
  "batch_size": 256,
  "num_batches": 122,
  "top_maps": ["canyon of the dead v1", "..."],
  "strata_counts": {
    "GLA_vs_GLA__long__canyon_of_the_dead_v1__1": 412,
    "...": 0
  },
  "batches": [
    {"batch_id": 0, "replay_ids": ["369445", "369389", "..."]},
    "..."
  ]
}
```

---

## Files Affected

| Path | Action |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/analyze_replays.py` | New |
| `scripts/create_batches.py` | New |
| `data/replay_metadata.json` | Generated (gitignored) |
| `data/batches.json` | Generated (gitignored) |

# Replay Metadata & Stratified Batching — Design

> Created: 2026-04-03
> Phase: 2b (M4 Mac scope)
> Status: Approved

---

## Problem

35,998 downloaded `.rep` files need to be analyzed before training so that the
`ILTrainer` can draw balanced mini-batches across faction matchups, game
durations, maps, and game modes. Without stratification the trainer will
overfit to GLA-heavy, long-game, single-map replays (the majority in the
GameReplays.org dataset).

---

## Solution

Two standalone scripts that form a pre-training data preparation pipeline:

```
replays/dataset/*.rep
        │
        ▼
scripts/analyze_replays.py  ─►  data/replay_metadata.json
                                        │
                                        ▼
                              scripts/create_batches.py  ─►  data/batches.json
```

Both scripts are self-contained — they add `src/` to `sys.path` and import
`zh_copilot.replay.*` directly. No package installation required.

---

## Script 1 — `scripts/analyze_replays.py`

### What it does

Scans a directory of `.rep` files, parses each one, and writes a JSON metadata
index.

### CLI

```
python scripts/analyze_replays.py [--replays replays/dataset] [--output data/replay_metadata.json] [--force]
```

- `--replays`  Directory containing `.rep` files (default: `replays/dataset`)
- `--output`   Output path (default: `data/replay_metadata.json`)
- `--force`    Re-analyze all files even if already indexed

### Metadata record schema

| Field            | Type         | Description |
|------------------|--------------|-------------|
| `replay_id`      | str          | File stem (e.g. `"369445"`) |
| `path`           | str          | Relative path to `.rep` file |
| `md5`            | str          | MD5 hex digest of raw file bytes |
| `duration_s`     | float        | Game length in seconds |
| `duration_bucket`| str          | `"short"` <5min / `"medium"` 5–20min / `"long"` >20min |
| `game_mode`      | int          | `original_game_mode` from header |
| `num_players`    | int          | Human slot count |
| `is_1v1`         | bool         | True if exactly 2 human players |
| `map_path`       | str          | Normalized map name from game options |
| `factions`       | list[str]    | Faction per human player |
| `matchup`        | str          | Sorted canonical key e.g. `"America_vs_GLA"` |
| `version`        | int          | `version_number` from header |
| `valid`          | bool         | Passes `is_valid_for_training` filter |
| `invalid_reason` | str \| null  | Reason if invalid: `"desync"`, `"too_short"`, `"disconnection"`, `"too_few_commands"`, `"duplicate"`, `"parse_error:<msg>"` |

### Output file shape

```json
{
  "generated_at": "2026-04-03T10:00:00+00:00",
  "replays_dir": "replays/dataset",
  "total": 35998,
  "valid": 31240,
  "replays": [ ... ]
}
```

### Deduplication

After scanning, any record whose `md5` appears more than once is marked
`valid: false`, `invalid_reason: "duplicate"` — only the first occurrence
(lowest replay_id) is kept valid. The metadata file is the single source of
truth for dedup.

### Resumption

Without `--force`, already-indexed `replay_id` values are skipped. The script
merges new records into an existing output file.

---

## Script 2 — `scripts/create_batches.py`

### What it does

Reads `data/replay_metadata.json`, takes only `valid: true` records, and
distributes them into stratified batches that each contain proportional
coverage of every stratum.

### CLI

```
python scripts/create_batches.py [--metadata data/replay_metadata.json] [--output data/batches.json] [--batch-size 256] [--top-maps 20] [--seed 42]
```

### Stratum key

```
{matchup}__{duration_bucket}__{map_bucket}__{game_mode}
```

- `map_bucket`: top-N most frequent map names kept as-is; remainder → `"other"`
- Map name is the final path component, lowercased, with `[rank]` prefix stripped

### Stratification algorithm

1. Shuffle IDs within each stratum (seeded for reproducibility)
2. For each stratum, assign `ids[i]` to `batches[i % num_batches]` — guarantees
   proportional representation regardless of stratum size
3. `num_batches = len(valid_replays) // batch_size`

### Output file shape

```json
{
  "generated_at": "2026-04-03T10:00:00+00:00",
  "batch_size": 256,
  "num_batches": 122,
  "top_maps": ["canyon of the dead v1", "..."],
  "strata_counts": {
    "GLA_vs_GLA__long__canyon_of_the_dead_v1__1": 412,
    "...": 0
  },
  "batches": [
    {"batch_id": 0, "replay_ids": ["369445", "369389", "..."]},
    "..."
  ]
}
```

---

## Files Affected

| Path | Action |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/analyze_replays.py` | New |
| `scripts/create_batches.py` | New |
| `data/replay_metadata.json` | Generated (gitignored) |
| `data/batches.json` | Generated (gitignored) |
| `tests/data/test_analyze_replays.py` | New |
| `tests/data/test_create_batches.py` | New |
| `docs/specs/2026-04-03-replay-metadata-batching-design.md` | This file |
| `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md` | Updated checkboxes |

---

## Future Integration

When Phase 1 (headless engine) is complete, the `ILTrainer` will read
`data/batches.json` to schedule which replays to run through the engine each
epoch. `replay_metadata.json` will be used by the dataset pipeline to attach
faction/map/phase labels to HDF5 records.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Scan a directory of .rep files, parse each one, and write replay_metadata.json.

Usage:
    python scripts/analyze_replays.py
    python scripts/analyze_replays.py --replays replays/dataset --output data/replay_metadata.json
    python scripts/analyze_replays.py --force   # re-analyze all, ignore existing index
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import json
import sys
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path

# Allow running from repo root without installing the package
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent / "src"))

from zh_copilot.replay.analysis import is_valid_for_training
from zh_copilot.replay.game_options import parse_game_options
from zh_copilot.replay.parser import parse_replay_bytes

try:
    from tqdm import tqdm as _tqdm
    def progress(it, **kw):  # type: ignore[return]
        return _tqdm(it, **kw)
except ImportError:
    def progress(it, total=None, desc=None, **_):  # type: ignore[misc]
        if desc:
            print(desc, flush=True)
        return it


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _md5(data: bytes) -> str:
    return hashlib.md5(data).hexdigest()


def _duration_bucket(seconds: float) -> str:
    if seconds < 300:
        return "short"
    if seconds < 1200:
        return "medium"
    return "long"


def _invalid_reason(rep) -> str | None:  # type: ignore[return]
    if rep.header.desync_game:
        return "desync"
    if rep.header.duration_seconds < 60.0:
        return "too_short"
    if any(rep.header.player_discons):
        return "disconnection"
    if len(rep.gameplay_commands) < 20:
        return "too_few_commands"
    return "unknown"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Core per-file analysis
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def analyze_file(path: Path) -> dict:
    raw = path.read_bytes()
    md5 = _md5(raw)
    replay_id = path.stem

    try:
        rep = parse_replay_bytes(raw)
    except Exception as exc:
        return {
            "replay_id": replay_id,
            "path": str(path),
            "md5": md5,
            "valid": False,
            "invalid_reason": f"parse_error:{exc}",
        }

    info = parse_game_options(rep.header.game_options)
    human_factions = [s.faction for s in info.slots if s.is_human]
    matchup = "_vs_".join(sorted(human_factions)) if human_factions else "unknown"

    valid = is_valid_for_training(rep)
    return {
        "replay_id": replay_id,
        "path": str(path),
        "md5": md5,
        "duration_s": rep.header.duration_seconds,
        "duration_bucket": _duration_bucket(rep.header.duration_seconds),
        "game_mode": rep.header.original_game_mode,
        "num_players": info.num_players,
        "is_1v1": info.is_1v1,
        "map_path": info.map_path,
        "factions": human_factions,
        "matchup": matchup,
        "version": rep.header.version_number,
        "valid": valid,
        "invalid_reason": None if valid else _invalid_reason(rep),
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Deduplication
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _mark_duplicates(records: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    """Keep the first occurrence of each md5; mark the rest as duplicate."""
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for rec in records:
        md5 = rec.get("md5", "")
        if not md5:
            continue
        if md5 in seen:
            rec["valid"] = False
            rec["invalid_reason"] = "duplicate"
        else:
            seen.add(md5)
    return records


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Analyze .rep replay files and write metadata JSON index."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--replays",
        default="replays/dataset",
        help="Directory containing .rep files (default: replays/dataset)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default="data/replay_metadata.json",
        help="Output JSON path (default: data/replay_metadata.json)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--force",
        action="store_true",
        help="Re-analyze all files even if already indexed",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    replays_dir = Path(args.replays)
    output_path = Path(args.output)

    if not replays_dir.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: replays directory not found: {replays_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    rep_files = sorted(replays_dir.glob("*.rep"))
    if not rep_files:
        print(f"No .rep files found in {replays_dir}")
        return

    # Load existing index for resumption
    existing: dict[str, dict] = {}
    if not args.force and output_path.exists():
        try:
            prev = json.loads(output_path.read_text())
            existing = {r["replay_id"]: r for r in prev.get("replays", [])}
            print(f"Resuming: {len(existing)} already indexed, skipping.")
        except Exception as exc:
            print(f"Warning: could not load existing index ({exc}), re-analyzing all.")

    records: list[dict] = list(existing.values())
    to_analyze = [f for f in rep_files if f.stem not in existing]

    print(f"Found {len(rep_files)} .rep files. Analyzing {len(to_analyze)} new files.")

    for path in progress(to_analyze, total=len(to_analyze), desc="Analyzing replays"):
        records.append(analyze_file(path))

    # Sort by replay_id for deterministic output
    records.sort(key=lambda r: r["replay_id"])

    # Dedup by MD5 (marks later occurrences invalid)
    records = _mark_duplicates(records)

    valid_count = sum(1 for r in records if r.get("valid"))
    output = {
        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "replays_dir": str(replays_dir),
        "total": len(records),
        "valid": valid_count,
        "replays": records,
    }

    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    output_path.write_text(json.dumps(output, indent=2))
    print(f"Written {len(records)} records ({valid_count} valid) to {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Read replay_metadata.json, stratify valid replays, and write batches.json.

Stratification dimensions: faction matchup, duration bucket, map, game mode.
Each batch receives proportional coverage of every stratum via round-robin
assignment within each stratum.

Usage:
    python scripts/create_batches.py
    python scripts/create_batches.py --batch-size 128 --top-maps 30 --seed 0
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import random
import sys
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _normalize_map_name(map_path: str) -> str:
    """Extract the final component of a map path and strip common prefixes."""
    name = Path(map_path).name.lower()
    for prefix in ("[rank] ", "[rank]"):
        if name.startswith(prefix):
            name = name[len(prefix):]
    return name.strip()


def _stratum_key(record: dict, top_maps: set[str]) -> str:
    map_name = _normalize_map_name(record.get("map_path", ""))
    map_bucket = map_name if map_name in top_maps else "other"
    return f"{record['matchup']}__{record['duration_bucket']}__{map_bucket}__{record['game_mode']}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Stratified batch builder
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_batches(
    valid_records: list[dict],
    batch_size: int,
    top_maps_n: int,
    seed: int,
) -> tuple[list[list[str]], dict[str, int], list[str]]:
    """Return (batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list).

    Each batch is a list of replay_id strings.
    Stratification: for each stratum, IDs are shuffled then assigned
    round-robin across batches (ids[i] -> batches[i % num_batches]).
    This guarantees every batch has proportional coverage of every stratum.
    """
    if not valid_records:
        return [], {}, []

    # Determine top-N maps by frequency
    map_counts: Counter[str] = Counter(
        _normalize_map_name(r.get("map_path", "")) for r in valid_records
    )
    top_maps: set[str] = {m for m, _ in map_counts.most_common(top_maps_n)}
    top_maps_list = sorted(top_maps)

    # Group by stratum
    strata: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for r in valid_records:
        key = _stratum_key(r, top_maps)
        strata[key].append(r["replay_id"])

    strata_counts = {k: len(v) for k, v in sorted(strata.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])}

    num_batches = max(1, len(valid_records) // batch_size)
    batches: list[list[str]] = [[] for _ in range(num_batches)]

    rng = random.Random(seed)

    # Round-robin assignment within each stratum
    for ids in strata.values():
        shuffled = ids[:]
        rng.shuffle(shuffled)
        for i, replay_id in enumerate(shuffled):
            batches[i % num_batches].append(replay_id)

    # Shuffle within each batch so stratum order doesn't leak through
    for batch in batches:
        rng.shuffle(batch)

    return batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Create stratified training batches from replay metadata."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--metadata",
        default="data/replay_metadata.json",
        help="Path to replay_metadata.json (default: data/replay_metadata.json)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default="data/batches.json",
        help="Output path for batches.json (default: data/batches.json)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--batch-size",
        type=int,
        default=256,
        help="Replays per batch (default: 256)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top-maps",
        type=int,
        default=20,
        help="Number of top maps to track as own bucket (default: 20)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=42,
        help="Random seed for reproducibility (default: 42)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    metadata_path = Path(args.metadata)
    output_path = Path(args.output)

    if not metadata_path.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: metadata file not found: {metadata_path}", file=sys.stderr)
        print("Run scripts/analyze_replays.py first.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    metadata = json.loads(metadata_path.read_text())
    all_records = metadata.get("replays", [])
    valid_records = [r for r in all_records if r.get("valid")]

    print(f"Total records: {len(all_records)}, valid: {len(valid_records)}")

    if not valid_records:
        print("No valid replays found. Nothing to batch.")
        return

    batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list = build_batches(
        valid_records,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        top_maps_n=args.top_maps,
        seed=args.seed,
    )

    output = {
        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "metadata_source": str(metadata_path),
        "batch_size": args.batch_size,
        "num_batches": len(batches),
        "total_valid_replays": len(valid_records),
        "top_maps": top_maps_list,
        "strata_counts": strata_counts,
        "batches": [
            {"batch_id": i, "replay_ids": ids}
            for i, ids in enumerate(batches)
        ],
    }

    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    output_path.write_text(json.dumps(output, indent=2))
    print(
        f"Written {len(batches)} batches "
        f"(~{args.batch_size} replays each) "
        f"across {len(strata_counts)} strata to {output_path}"
    )

    # Print top-5 strata summary
    print("\nTop 5 strata:")
    for key, count in list(strata_counts.items())[:5]:
        print(f"  {count:5d}  {key}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Read replay_metadata.json, stratify valid replays, and write batches.json.

Stratification dimensions: faction matchup, duration bucket, map, game mode.
Each batch receives proportional coverage of every stratum via round-robin
assignment within each stratum.

Usage:
    python scripts/create_batches.py
    python scripts/create_batches.py --batch-size 128 --top-maps 30 --seed 0
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import random
import sys
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _normalize_map_name(map_path: str) -> str:
    """Extract the final component of a map path and strip common prefixes."""
    name = Path(map_path).name.lower()
    for prefix in ("[rank] ", "[rank]"):
        if name.startswith(prefix):
            name = name[len(prefix):]
    return name.strip()


def _stratum_key(record: dict, top_maps: set[str]) -> str:
    map_name = _normalize_map_name(record.get("map_path", ""))
    map_bucket = map_name if map_name in top_maps else "other"
    return f"{record['matchup']}__{record['duration_bucket']}__{map_bucket}__{record['game_mode']}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Stratified batch builder
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_batches(
    valid_records: list[dict],
    batch_size: int,
    top_maps_n: int,
    seed: int,
) -> tuple[list[list[str]], dict[str, int], list[str]]:
    """Return (batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list).

    Each batch is a list of replay_id strings.
    Stratification: for each stratum, IDs are shuffled then assigned
    round-robin across batches (ids[i] -> batches[i % num_batches]).
    This guarantees every batch has proportional coverage of every stratum.
    """
    if not valid_records:
        return [], {}, []

    # Determine top-N maps by frequency
    map_counts: Counter[str] = Counter(
        _normalize_map_name(r.get("map_path", "")) for r in valid_records
    )
    top_maps: set[str] = {m for m, _ in map_counts.most_common(top_maps_n)}
    top_maps_list = sorted(top_maps)

    # Group by stratum
    strata: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for r in valid_records:
        key = _stratum_key(r, top_maps)
        strata[key].append(r["replay_id"])

    strata_counts = {k: len(v) for k, v in sorted(strata.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])}

    num_batches = max(1, len(valid_records) // batch_size)
    batches: list[list[str]] = [[] for _ in range(num_batches)]

    rng = random.Random(seed)

    # Round-robin assignment within each stratum
    for ids in strata.values():
        shuffled = ids[:]
        rng.shuffle(shuffled)
        for i, replay_id in enumerate(shuffled):
            batches[i % num_batches].append(replay_id)

    # Shuffle within each batch so stratum order doesn't leak through
    for batch in batches:
        rng.shuffle(batch)

    return batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Create stratified training batches from replay metadata."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--metadata",
        default="data/replay_metadata.json",
        help="Path to replay_metadata.json (default: data/replay_metadata.json)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default="data/batches.json",
        help="Output path for batches.json (default: data/batches.json)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--batch-size",
        type=int,
        default=256,
        help="Replays per batch (default: 256)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top-maps",
        type=int,
        default=20,
        help="Number of top maps to track as own bucket (default: 20)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=42,
        help="Random seed for reproducibility (default: 42)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    metadata_path = Path(args.metadata)
    output_path = Path(args.output)

    if not metadata_path.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: metadata file not found: {metadata_path}", file=sys.stderr)
        print("Run scripts/analyze_replays.py first.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    metadata = json.loads(metadata_path.read_text())
    all_records = metadata.get("replays", [])
    valid_records = [r for r in all_records if r.get("valid")]

    print(f"Total records: {len(all_records)}, valid: {len(valid_records)}")

    if not valid_records:
        print("No valid replays found. Nothing to batch.")
        return

    batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list = build_batches(
        valid_records,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        top_maps_n=args.top_maps,
        seed=args.seed,
    )

    output = {
        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "metadata_source": str(metadata_path),
        "batch_size": args.batch_size,
        "num_batches": len(batches),
        "total_valid_replays": len(valid_records),
        "top_maps": top_maps_list,
        "strata_counts": strata_counts,
        "batches": [
            {"batch_id": i, "replay_ids": ids}
            for i, ids in enumerate(batches)
        ],
    }

    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    output_path.write_text(json.dumps(output, indent=2))
    print(
        f"Written {len(batches)} batches "
        f"(~{args.batch_size} replays each) "
        f"across {len(strata_counts)} strata to {output_path}"
    )

    # Print top-5 strata summary
    print("\nTop 5 strata:")
    for key, count in list(strata_counts.items())[:5]:
        print(f"  {count:5d}  {key}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# tests/data/test_analyze_replays.py
"""Tests for scripts/analyze_replays.py logic."""

import hashlib
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

# Import helpers directly from the script (it adds src/ to sys.path itself)
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / "src"))
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / "scripts"))

from analyze_replays import (
    _duration_bucket,
    _mark_duplicates,
    analyze_file,
)

EXAMPLES_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / "replays" / "examples"
EXAMPLE_REPS = sorted(EXAMPLES_DIR.glob("*.rep"))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Unit tests — helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_duration_bucket_short():
    assert _duration_bucket(60) == "short"
    assert _duration_bucket(299) == "short"


def test_duration_bucket_medium():
    assert _duration_bucket(300) == "medium"
    assert _duration_bucket(1199) == "medium"


def test_duration_bucket_long():
    assert _duration_bucket(1200) == "long"
    assert _duration_bucket(9999) == "long"


def test_mark_duplicates_keeps_first():
    records = [
        {"replay_id": "a", "md5": "abc", "valid": True, "invalid_reason": None},
        {"replay_id": "b", "md5": "abc", "valid": True, "invalid_reason": None},
        {"replay_id": "c", "md5": "xyz", "valid": True, "invalid_reason": None},
    ]
    result = _mark_duplicates(records)
    assert result[0]["valid"] is True
    assert result[1]["valid"] is False
    assert result[1]["invalid_reason"] == "duplicate"
    assert result[2]["valid"] is True


def test_mark_duplicates_no_duplicates():
    records = [
        {"replay_id": "a", "md5": "aaa", "valid": True, "invalid_reason": None},
        {"replay_id": "b", "md5": "bbb", "valid": True, "invalid_reason": None},
    ]
    result = _mark_duplicates(records)
    assert all(r["valid"] for r in result)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Integration tests — real .rep files
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.mark.skipif(not EXAMPLE_REPS, reason="No example .rep files found")
@pytest.mark.parametrize("rep_path", EXAMPLE_REPS, ids=[p.name for p in EXAMPLE_REPS])
def test_analyze_file_required_fields(rep_path):
    """Every .rep file must produce a record with all required fields."""
    record = analyze_file(rep_path)

    required = [
        "replay_id", "path", "md5", "valid", "invalid_reason",
    ]
    for field in required:
        assert field in record, f"Missing field: {field}"

    assert record["replay_id"] == rep_path.stem
    assert len(record["md5"]) == 32  # MD5 hex digest is always 32 chars


@pytest.mark.skipif(not EXAMPLE_REPS, reason="No example .rep files found")
@pytest.mark.parametrize("rep_path", EXAMPLE_REPS, ids=[p.name for p in EXAMPLE_REPS])
def test_analyze_file_valid_records_have_full_metadata(rep_path):
    """Valid records must carry all metadata fields."""
    record = analyze_file(rep_path)
    if not record.get("valid"):
        pytest.skip(f"Replay {rep_path.name} is invalid ({record.get('invalid_reason')}), skipping metadata check")

    metadata_fields = [
        "duration_s", "duration_bucket", "game_mode", "num_players",
        "is_1v1", "map_path", "factions", "matchup", "version",
    ]
    for field in metadata_fields:
        assert field in record, f"Missing metadata field: {field}"

    assert record["duration_bucket"] in ("short", "medium", "long")
    assert isinstance(record["factions"], list)
    assert isinstance(record["duration_s"], float)


@pytest.mark.skipif(not EXAMPLE_REPS, reason="No example .rep files found")
def test_analyze_file_md5_matches_file_content():
    """MD5 in record must match actual file bytes."""
    rep_path = EXAMPLE_REPS[0]
    record = analyze_file(rep_path)
    expected_md5 = hashlib.md5(rep_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
    assert record["md5"] == expected_md5


@pytest.mark.skipif(not EXAMPLE_REPS, reason="No example .rep files found")
def test_analyze_file_parse_error_returns_valid_false(tmp_path):
    """A corrupt .rep file must return valid=False with a parse_error reason."""
    corrupt = tmp_path / "corrupt.rep"
    corrupt.write_bytes(b"NOTAREP\x00\x00\x00")
    record = analyze_file(corrupt)
    assert record["valid"] is False
    assert record["invalid_reason"].startswith("parse_error:")

# tests/data/test_create_batches.py
"""Tests for scripts/create_batches.py logic."""

import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / "scripts"))

from create_batches import _normalize_map_name, _stratum_key, build_batches


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Unit tests — helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_normalize_map_name_strips_rank_prefix():
    assert _normalize_map_name("userdata/maps/[rank] canyon of the dead v1") == "canyon of the dead v1"


def test_normalize_map_name_no_prefix():
    assert _normalize_map_name("userdata/maps/alpine assault") == "alpine assault"


def test_normalize_map_name_empty():
    assert _normalize_map_name("") == ""


def test_stratum_key_known_map():
    record = {
        "matchup": "GLA_vs_GLA",
        "duration_bucket": "long",
        "map_path": "userdata/maps/[rank] canyon of the dead v1",
        "game_mode": 1,
    }
    top_maps = {"canyon of the dead v1"}
    key = _stratum_key(record, top_maps)
    assert key == "GLA_vs_GLA__long__canyon of the dead v1__1"


def test_stratum_key_unknown_map_bins_to_other():
    record = {
        "matchup": "America_vs_GLA",
        "duration_bucket": "medium",
        "map_path": "userdata/maps/obscure_map_xyz",
        "game_mode": 1,
    }
    top_maps = {"canyon of the dead v1"}
    key = _stratum_key(record, top_maps)
    assert "__other__" in key


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Unit tests — build_batches
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_records(n: int, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="long",
                   map_path="maps/canyon", game_mode=1) -> list[dict]:
    return [
        {
            "replay_id": str(i),
            "matchup": matchup,
            "duration_bucket": duration_bucket,
            "map_path": map_path,
            "game_mode": game_mode,
            "valid": True,
        }
        for i in range(n)
    ]


def test_build_batches_returns_correct_count():
    records = _make_records(100)
    batches, strata, top_maps = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(batches) == 10


def test_build_batches_all_ids_present():
    records = _make_records(50)
    batches, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    all_ids = [rid for batch in batches for rid in batch]
    assert sorted(all_ids) == [str(i) for i in range(50)]


def test_build_batches_no_duplicate_ids():
    records = _make_records(60)
    batches, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    all_ids = [rid for batch in batches for rid in batch]
    assert len(all_ids) == len(set(all_ids))


def test_build_batches_empty_returns_empty():
    batches, strata, top_maps = build_batches([], batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert batches == []
    assert strata == {}
    assert top_maps == []


def test_build_batches_single_record():
    records = _make_records(1)
    batches, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(batches) == 1
    assert batches[0] == ["0"]


def test_build_batches_strata_counts_sum_to_total():
    records = (
        _make_records(30, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA") +
        _make_records(20, matchup="America_vs_GLA") +
        _make_records(10, matchup="America_vs_China")
    )
    batches, strata_counts, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert sum(strata_counts.values()) == 60


def test_build_batches_multi_strata_all_present_in_each_batch():
    """With enough replays, every stratum should appear in every batch."""
    n_per_stratum = 20
    records = (
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="long") +
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="America_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="medium") +
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="China_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="short")
    )
    batches, strata_counts, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(strata_counts) == 3
    # Each stratum has 20 items across 6 batches → some batches get 3, some 4
    # Total must still equal 60
    assert sum(len(b) for b in batches) == 60


def test_build_batches_reproducible_with_same_seed():
    records = _make_records(100)
    b1, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=99)
    b2, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=99)
    assert b1 == b2


def test_build_batches_different_seeds_differ():
    records = _make_records(100)
    b1, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=1)
    b2, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=2)
    assert b1 != b2

def test_build_batches_empty_returns_empty():
    batches, strata, top_maps = build_batches([], batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert batches == []
    assert strata == {}
    assert top_maps == []


def test_build_batches_single_record():
    records = _make_records(1)
    batches, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(batches) == 1
    assert batches[0] == ["0"]


def test_build_batches_strata_counts_sum_to_total():
    records = (
        _make_records(30, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA") +
        _make_records(20, matchup="America_vs_GLA") +
        _make_records(10, matchup="America_vs_China")
    )
    batches, strata_counts, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert sum(strata_counts.values()) == 60


def test_build_batches_multi_strata_all_present_in_each_batch():
    """With enough replays, every stratum should appear in every batch."""
    n_per_stratum = 20
    records = (
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="long") +
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="America_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="medium") +
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="China_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="short")
    )
    batches, strata_counts, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(strata_counts) == 3
    # Each stratum has 20 items across 6 batches → some batches get 3, some 4
    # Total must still equal 60
    assert sum(len(b) for b in batches) == 60


def test_build_batches_reproducible_with_same_seed():
    records = _make_records(100)
    b1, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=99)
    b2, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=99)
    assert b1 == b2


def test_build_batches_different_seeds_differ():
    records = _make_records(100)
    b1, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=1)
    b2, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=2)
    assert b1 != b2

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Read replay_metadata.json, stratify valid replays, and write batches.json.

Stratification dimensions: faction matchup, duration bucket, map, game mode.
Each batch receives proportional coverage of every stratum via round-robin
assignment within each stratum.

Usage:
    python scripts/create_batches.py
    python scripts/create_batches.py --batch-size 128 --top-maps 30 --seed 0
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import random
import sys
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _normalize_map_name(map_path: str) -> str:
    """Extract the final component of a map path and strip common prefixes."""
    name = Path(map_path).name.lower()
    for prefix in ("[rank] ", "[rank]"):
        if name.startswith(prefix):
            name = name[len(prefix):]
    return name.strip()


def _stratum_key(record: dict, top_maps: set[str]) -> str:
    map_name = _normalize_map_name(record.get("map_path", ""))
    map_bucket = map_name if map_name in top_maps else "other"
    return f"{record['matchup']}__{record['duration_bucket']}__{map_bucket}__{record['game_mode']}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Stratified batch builder
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_batches(
    valid_records: list[dict],
    batch_size: int,
    top_maps_n: int,
    seed: int,
) -> tuple[list[list[str]], dict[str, int], list[str]]:
    """Return (batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list).

    Each batch is a list of replay_id strings.
    Stratification: for each stratum, IDs are shuffled then assigned
    round-robin across batches (ids[i] -> batches[i % num_batches]).
    This guarantees every batch has proportional coverage of every stratum.
    """
    if not valid_records:
        return [], {}, []

    # Determine top-N maps by frequency
    map_counts: Counter[str] = Counter(
        _normalize_map_name(r.get("map_path", "")) for r in valid_records
    )
    top_maps: set[str] = {m for m, _ in map_counts.most_common(top_maps_n)}
    top_maps_list = sorted(top_maps)

    # Group by stratum
    strata: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for r in valid_records:
        key = _stratum_key(r, top_maps)
        strata[key].append(r["replay_id"])

    strata_counts = {k: len(v) for k, v in sorted(strata.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])}

    num_batches = max(1, len(valid_records) // batch_size)
    batches: list[list[str]] = [[] for _ in range(num_batches)]

    rng = random.Random(seed)

    # Round-robin assignment within each stratum
    for ids in strata.values():
        shuffled = ids[:]
        rng.shuffle(shuffled)
        for i, replay_id in enumerate(shuffled):
            batches[i % num_batches].append(replay_id)

    # Shuffle within each batch so stratum order doesn't leak through
    for batch in batches:
        rng.shuffle(batch)

    return batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Create stratified training batches from replay metadata."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--metadata",
        default="data/replay_metadata.json",
        help="Path to replay_metadata.json (default: data/replay_metadata.json)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default="data/batches.json",
        help="Output path for batches.json (default: data/batches.json)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--batch-size",
        type=int,
        default=256,
        help="Replays per batch (default: 256)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top-maps",
        type=int,
        default=20,
        help="Number of top maps to track as own bucket (default: 20)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=42,
        help="Random seed for reproducibility (default: 42)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    metadata_path = Path(args.metadata)
    output_path = Path(args.output)

    if not metadata_path.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: metadata file not found: {metadata_path}", file=sys.stderr)
        print("Run scripts/analyze_replays.py first.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    metadata = json.loads(metadata_path.read_text())
    all_records = metadata.get("replays", [])
    valid_records = [r for r in all_records if r.get("valid")]

    print(f"Total records: {len(all_records)}, valid: {len(valid_records)}")

    if not valid_records:
        print("No valid replays found. Nothing to batch.")
        return

    batches, strata_counts, top_maps_list = build_batches(
        valid_records,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        top_maps_n=args.top_maps,
        seed=args.seed,
    )

    output = {
        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "metadata_source": str(metadata_path),
        "batch_size": args.batch_size,
        "num_batches": len(batches),
        "total_valid_replays": len(valid_records),
        "top_maps": top_maps_list,
        "strata_counts": strata_counts,
        "batches": [
            {"batch_id": i, "replay_ids": ids}
            for i, ids in enumerate(batches)
        ],
    }

    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    output_path.write_text(json.dumps(output, indent=2))
    print(
        f"Written {len(batches)} batches "
        f"(~{args.batch_size} replays each) "
        f"across {len(strata_counts)} strata to {output_path}"
    )

    # Print top-5 strata summary
    print("\nTop 5 strata:")
    for key, count in list(strata_counts.items())[:5]:
        print(f"  {count:5d}  {key}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# tests/data/test_create_batches.py
"""Tests for scripts/create_batches.py logic."""

import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / "scripts"))

from create_batches import _normalize_map_name, _stratum_key, build_batches


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Unit tests — helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_normalize_map_name_strips_rank_prefix():
    assert _normalize_map_name("userdata/maps/[rank] canyon of the dead v1") == "canyon of the dead v1"


def test_normalize_map_name_no_prefix():
    assert _normalize_map_name("userdata/maps/alpine assault") == "alpine assault"


def test_normalize_map_name_empty():
    assert _normalize_map_name("") == ""


def test_stratum_key_known_map():
    record = {
        "matchup": "GLA_vs_GLA",
        "duration_bucket": "long",
        "map_path": "userdata/maps/[rank] canyon of the dead v1",
        "game_mode": 1,
    }
    top_maps = {"canyon of the dead v1"}
    key = _stratum_key(record, top_maps)
    assert key == "GLA_vs_GLA__long__canyon of the dead v1__1"


def test_stratum_key_unknown_map_bins_to_other():
    record = {
        "matchup": "America_vs_GLA",
        "duration_bucket": "medium",
        "map_path": "userdata/maps/obscure_map_xyz",
        "game_mode": 1,
    }
    top_maps = {"canyon of the dead v1"}
    key = _stratum_key(record, top_maps)
    assert "__other__" in key


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Unit tests — build_batches
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_records(n: int, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="long",
                   map_path="maps/canyon", game_mode=1) -> list[dict]:
    return [
        {
            "replay_id": str(i),
            "matchup": matchup,
            "duration_bucket": duration_bucket,
            "map_path": map_path,
            "game_mode": game_mode,
            "valid": True,
        }
        for i in range(n)
    ]


def test_build_batches_returns_correct_count():
    records = _make_records(100)
    batches, strata, top_maps = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(batches) == 10


def test_build_batches_all_ids_present():
    records = _make_records(50)
    batches, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    all_ids = [rid for batch in batches for rid in batch]
    assert sorted(all_ids) == [str(i) for i in range(50)]


def test_build_batches_no_duplicate_ids():
    records = _make_records(60)
    batches, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    all_ids = [rid for batch in batches for rid in batch]
    assert len(all_ids) == len(set(all_ids))


def test_build_batches_empty_returns_empty():
    batches, strata, top_maps = build_batches([], batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert batches == []
    assert strata == {}
    assert top_maps == []


def test_build_batches_single_record():
    records = _make_records(1)
    batches, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(batches) == 1
    assert batches[0] == ["0"]


def test_build_batches_strata_counts_sum_to_total():
    records = (
        _make_records(30, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA") +
        _make_records(20, matchup="America_vs_GLA") +
        _make_records(10, matchup="America_vs_China")
    )
    batches, strata_counts, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert sum(strata_counts.values()) == 60


def test_build_batches_multi_strata_all_present_in_each_batch():
    """With enough replays, every stratum should appear in every batch."""
    n_per_stratum = 20
    records = (
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="GLA_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="long") +
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="America_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="medium") +
        _make_records(n_per_stratum, matchup="China_vs_GLA", duration_bucket="short")
    )
    batches, strata_counts, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=42)
    assert len(strata_counts) == 3
    # Each stratum has 20 items across 6 batches → some batches get 3, some 4
    # Total must still equal 60
    assert sum(len(b) for b in batches) == 60


def test_build_batches_reproducible_with_same_seed():
    records = _make_records(100)
    b1, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=99)
    b2, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=99)
    assert b1 == b2


def test_build_batches_different_seeds_differ():
    records = _make_records(100)
    b1, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=1)
    b2, _, _ = build_batches(records, batch_size=10, top_maps_n=5, seed=2)
    assert b1 != b2

# ZH-Copilot Implementation Plan

> Last updated: 2026-03-30
> Status: Planning phase

---

## Phase 0: Project Setup
- [x] Initialize repository and project structure
  - [x] Git init, .gitignore, README

  - [x] Document game state structures and data types
- [ ] Set up development environment
  - [x] M4 Mac: Python, PyTorch, dev tools
  - [ ] Intel Mac: C++ compiler, build dependencies

## Phase 1: Headless Engine & Game Interface
- [ ] Build headless engine (no rendering)
  - [ ] Strip GameEngineDevice / DirectX dependencies
  - [ ] Replace with stubs (null renderer, null audio)
  - [ ] Compile on Intel Mac (x86)
  - [ ] Verify game logic runs without rendering
- [ ] Game State Extraction Layer (C++)
  - [ ] Map terrain extraction
  - [ ] Entity list extraction (units, buildings, resources)
  - [ ] Player state extraction (money, power, supply)
  - [ ] Fog of war state
  - [ ] Visibility masks per player
- [ ] Action Injection Layer (C++)
  - [ ] Build structure command
  - [ ] Train unit command
  - [ ] Move / attack-move / patrol commands
  - [ ] Use ability / general power commands
  - [ ] Sell / cancel commands
- [ ] Python Bindings (pybind11)
  - [ ] Expose state extraction as Python dict / numpy arrays
  - [ ] Expose action injection as Python functions
  - [ ] Gym-compatible environment wrapper
  - [ ] Multi-instance support (run N games in parallel)

- [x] Replay Parser (M4 scope complete)
  - [x] Reverse-engineer .rep file format (GENREP magic, header, command stream)

## Phase 3: AI Model
- [x] State Encoder
  - [x] Custom CNN for spatial map (128x128, ~40 channels)
  - [x] Transformer encoder for entity list (8 layers, 8 heads, max 1024 entities)
  - [x] MLP for global scalars
  - [x] Fusion layer
- [x] Temporal Module
  - [x] LSTM (512 hidden)
  - [x] Strategy encoder with learned archetypes
- [x] Action Decoder
  - [x] Action type head (softmax)
  - [x] Target pointer network (attention over entities)
  - [x] Spatial position head (64x64 probability map)
  - [ ] Multi-action repeat head
- [x] Value Head
  - [x] Win probability estimator (for RL)
- [ ] Model Infrastructure
  - [x] Config system for hyperparameters
  - [x] Checkpointing and model versioning
  - [ ] Inference optimization for deployment

## Phase 4: Imitation Learning (Replays)
- [x] Training Pipeline
  - [x] Dataloader for replay datasets
  - [x] Multi-task loss (action CE + pointer + spatial + value)
  - [ ] Auxiliary losses (opponent action prediction)
  - [x] Training loop with logging (W&B or TensorBoard)
  - [x] StratifiedBatchSampler (group by faction, strategy_label, game_phase; max 40% per stratum)
  - [x] ILTrainer uses StratifiedBatchSampler instead of random shuffle
- [x] Strategy Clustering
  - [x] Cluster replay openings into archetypes per faction matchup
  - [x] Label replays with strategy embeddings
  - [ ] Train strategy encoder
- [ ] Evaluation
  - [ ] Action prediction accuracy on held-out replays
  - [ ] Build order accuracy
  - [ ] Play vs built-in Easy/Medium AI
  - [ ] Target: beat Medium AI consistently

## Phase 5: Reinforcement Learning
- [ ] RL vs Built-in AI
  - [ ] PPO implementation
  - [ ] Reward shaping (win/loss, resources, kills, map control)
  - [ ] Curriculum: Easy → Medium → Hard → Brutal
  - [ ] Parallel environment runner
  - [ ] Target: beat Brutal AI consistently
- [ ] Self-play League
  - [ ] League system (main agent + past snapshots + exploiters)
  - [ ] ELO tracking
  - [ ] Matchmaking between agents
  - [ ] Anti-forgetting mechanisms
  - [ ] Target: 70%+ vs good human players
- [ ] Multi-faction Training
  - [ ] Train across USA, China, GLA
  - [ ] Faction-specific strategy embeddings
  - [ ] Cross-faction matchup balancing

## Phase 6: Deployment — Screen Capture & Input
- [ ] Screen Capture Pipeline
  - [ ] Real-time screen capture (60 FPS)
  - [ ] Game window detection and cropping
- [ ] Computer Vision Models

- [ ] Input Emulation
  - [ ] Action-to-input mapping (model action → clicks/keys)
  - [ ] Camera management
  - [ ] Control group management
  - [ ] pyautogui or platform-native input

## Phase 7: Copilot UI
- [ ] Autopilot Mode
  - [ ] Full autonomous play
  - [ ] Status overlay (what the bot is doing)
- [ ] Copilot Mode (bot plays, human advises)
  - [ ] Strategy button panel (Attack, Defend, Rush, Farm, Turtle, Auto)
  - [ ] Strategy encoding → model conditioning
  - [ ] Status feedback to human
- [ ] Voice Input (later)
  - [ ] Speech-to-text integration
  - [ ] Command parsing
  - [ ] Map to strategy buttons

## Phase 8: Multi-mode Expansion
- [ ] Team Games (2v2, 3v3, 4v4)
  - [ ] Fine-tune on team replay data
  - [ ] Ally awareness and coordination
  - [ ] Shared resource strategies
- [ ] FFA
  - [ ] Multi-opponent threat assessment
  - [ ] Diplomacy-like behavior (target strongest player)
  - [ ] Survival-oriented strategy shifts
